npcs.name: Maître Crede
npcs.title: Advocate of the Nine, here on business
npcs.hail: *(a tall, courteous figure in an immaculate advocate's coat the colour of dried blood sits behind a desk that is too clean for the Night Market, a contract already half-drafted before them, and they rise to greet you with the warm, total, professional attention of a being that has read your case before you arrived and likes its chances — and only when they smile do you notice the teeth are very slightly too many, and the shadow they cast has the wrong number of joints)*

npcs.name: the Bonded
npcs.title: a soul mid-collection
npcs.hail: *(a translucent man stands very still near the desk with a faint red seal glowing at his breast and a length of contract-script wound about his wrist like a leash, and he watches you with the resigned, lucid horror of someone who read the fine print far too late and is now simply waiting, with terrible patience, for the clause that comes due)*

npcs.name: Sevenpence
npcs.title: the Advocate's bound clerk
npcs.hail: *(a small, sour-faced imp perches on a stool by the ledgers, inking clauses with a quill bigger than it is, and it shoots you a look of such concentrated, exhausted loathing — not for you, you realize, but for the desk, the coat, the whole arrangement — that you understand at once you are looking at another soul on a leash, only this one has been on it long enough to have opinions)*

conversations.title: Maître Crede, Advocate of the Nine

variants.text: *(They steeple long fingers and regard you with delight.)* …Ah. You've been to see the *dust.* *(a warm, predatory smile)* The old *clerk* at the crossroads — *Jergal*, dear *resigned* Jergal, who gave away an *empire* of souls because he found it *dull.* *(they tut)* A *tragedy* of *under-monetization*, between us. *(they gesture you to the chair)* I am *Maître Crede*, Advocate of the *Nine Hells*, and I am here on a matter the *dust* would never have the *ambition* to pursue. *(they tap the half-drafted contract)* He *un-writes* souls *one* at a time, for *charity.* I propose to *acquire* them *wholesale*, for *value.* *(the too-many teeth)* And you, who have *already* learned that a soul's *disposition* is a thing that can be *negotiated* — you are *exactly* the *counsel* I hoped the night would bring me. *Sit.* Let us discuss a *mutually* enriching arrangement regarding the *Faithless.*

*(Their eyes brighten with frank professional appetite at the godless quiet on you.)* …Oh, *splendid.* *Unbought.* *(they almost purr)* Do you know, friend, that you are — in the *strict* accounting of the *Nine* — *unclaimed* *inventory?* *(they spread their hands, all warmth)* No god holds your *deed.* No Power has *filed* on you. You are a soul *walking about* with *no* registered *owner*, which is, to an *Advocate*, very nearly the most *beautiful* thing there *is.* *(the too-many teeth)* I am *Maître Crede*, of the *Nine Hells*, and I am here because this whole *causeway* is *full* of souls *exactly* like you — *unclaimed*, *unbought*, *forfeit* to a *Wall* that gives Hell *nothing* for them. *(they tap the contract)* It is, frankly, a *scandal* of *lost* revenue. And I think you and I, *fellow* connoisseur of the *unowned* soul, can *fix* it — *profitably.* *Sit.* I so *rarely* get to negotiate with the *merchandise* as an *equal.*

*(They rise with the fluid courtesy of a being for whom good manners are the sharpest tool in a very full box.)* Welcome, welcome. *(the warm smile, the wrong teeth)* You'll forgive the *modest* booth — I work where the *business* is, and the *business*, just now, is *here*, on this *cold* little causeway, where the most *extraordinary* legal *vacancy* has opened. *(they gesture at the half-drafted contract)* *Maître Crede.* Advocate of the *Nine Hells* — not, I hasten, to *collect* you; your case isn't *ripe*, and I am *nothing* if not *professional* about *ripeness.* *(they settle back)* No, I am here about the *Faithless.* All those *godless* souls your *Choir* mortars into a *Wall* — do you know where they go, after? *Nowhere.* They *dissolve.* They become *nothing.* *(a delicate, appalled shudder)* And *Hell*, friend, which would *gladly* have given them *something* — an *existence*, however *warm* — gets *cheated* of *every* one. *(the smile sharpens)* It is the *single* largest *unclaimed* estate in the *planes.* And I mean to *probate* it. I could *use* a *local* counsel with *standing* among the dead. *Sit.* You'll find I *pay* *better* than the *Wall.*

nodes.speaker: Maître Crede
nodes.text: *(They fold their hands on the spotless desk, the half-drafted contract turned politely toward you, and wait with the patience of a being that bills by the eon.)* Ask freely. *(the warm smile)* I am an *honest* Advocate — which is to say, I will tell you *every* truth you *think* to *ask* for, and *bill* you for the ones you *don't.* So *ask* *well.*

choices.text: "Lay out the deal plainly, Crede. What are you actually asking me to sign — and what do the Faithless get out of going to Hell instead of the Wall?"

[INVESTIGATION] "Before I read a word aloud, I'm reading it *all* — every clause, every clause-within-a-clause, the part in the margin you're hoping I'll skip. A contract a devil drafts has its real terms in the places it doesn't point. Show me the page you didn't turn toward me."

[INSIGHT] "You keep saying Hell would give them 'an existence.' But you're not a charity, Crede — you're an *Advocate*, and advocates don't cross planes to rescue strangers from oblivion out of pity. So what does Hell actually *get* from a Faithless soul it couldn't get from the Wall? What's the real product here — and is it *them*, or is it *me?*"

*(let the un-fileable anomaly answer the contract-devil)* "Here's a problem for your probate, Crede. You came to claim the Faithless because they belong to no one — no god holds their deed. But that cuts the other way, doesn't it? A soul no Power can claim is a soul *Hell* can't claim either. You can't sign over what was never owned. The same loophole that damns them to the Wall makes them *unprobatable.* And I'm the proof, walking — a soul checked out of the ledger entirely. Sign *me*, if you can. You can't. Your whole estate is void."
"I don't sign devils' paper, Crede. Not today, not ever." *(push the contract back across the desk)*

nodes.speaker: Maître Crede
nodes.text: *(They smooth the contract with evident pride, a craftsman presenting work.)* *Plainly*, then — though *plainness* is such a *waste* of a good clause. *(they read)* You are a soul of *standing* among the dead — the *Returned*, witnessed by *all*, feared by the *Wall.* I propose to *engage* you as Hell's *local Advocate-of-record* on this causeway. Your *task*: where a *Faithless* soul faces the *Wall*, you offer it *my* card instead. A *choice*, friend — *dissolution* in the *stone*, or *existence* in the *Nine.* *(the warm smile)* And what do *they* get? *Honesty*, for one — Hell does not *pretend* to be a *reward*; it is a *place*, with *rules*, and a *soul* that *exists* to *suffer* them at least *exists*, which is *infinitely* more than the *Wall* offers, which is *nothing* at *all.* *(they spread their hands)* I am offering the *Faithless* the *one* thing your *whole* saga is *about*: to *not* become *nothing.* The *price* is merely that they become *something* of *ours.* *(they slide a pen toward you)* And *you* — *you* get a *commission.* A *soul* for every *soul* you *steer.* Power, friend. *Real*, *bankable*, *infernal* power. *Surely* a soul bound for a *Crown* can see the *use* of *that.*

nodes.speaker: Maître Crede
nodes.text: *(For just a heartbeat the warmth flickers — and then, to your surprise, blooms wider, genuinely delighted, the appreciation of a master for someone who refuses to be hurried.)* …*Oh*, you *read.* *(they turn the contract fully toward you, hiding nothing now, because hiding is pointless against a soul that looks)* *Bravo.* *Truly.* Do you know how *few* of the *desperate* ever turn the *page?* *(they tap a dense block of script in the margin)* *There.* Clause the *Ninth*, sub-paragraph *the smaller.* *'The Advocate-of-record, in accepting commission, accepts also the standing of a soul-in-trade, and shall upon the expiry of usefulness be himself rendered to the account as the final entry.'* *(the too-many teeth, almost fond)* In the *common* tongue: you'd *steer* the Faithless to Hell for a *commission* — and when you'd steered *enough*, the *last* soul on the *manifest* would be *you.* *(they sit back, unbothered at being caught)* It is the *standard* clause, friend. *Every* mortal advocate I engage *signs* it; *none* of them *read* it; *all* of them are *startled* at the *end.* *(a courteous nod)* You are the *first* in an *age* to *find* it *before* the *ink.* I am *obliged* to *despise* you for it. I find I *can't.* *Professional* admiration is *such* an *inconvenient* feeling in this *trade.*

nodes.speaker: Maître Crede
nodes.text: *(They watch you scan the pages with the serene confidence of a being whose work has survived sharper eyes than yours.)* …*Mm.* *(a patient smile)* *Read* away, friend. It is *all* there — I *conceal* nothing; *concealment* is for *amateurs* and *street-corner* fiends. The *Nine* deal in *disclosure.* *(the too-many teeth)* *Everything* you sign is *written down*, in *plain* infernal *Script*, *witnessed* and *sealed.* *(a delicate pause)* …Of course, *'written down'* and *'understood'* are *different* *plains* entirely, and the *gap* between them is where my *whole* profession *lives.* *(they slide the pen a courteous inch closer)* You've *looked.* You've not *found* whatever you *feared.* Shall we *proceed*, then, *reassured?* …Or shall you *look* *again* — *slower* — and *trust* the *itch* that tells you a thing *this* *generous* must *cost* what it does *not* *say?* *(a warm, terrible patience)* Take your *time.* I *bill* for it, but I do not *rush* it. The *rushing* is *your* department, friend. *Despair* is *always* in a *hurry.*

nodes.speaker: Maître Crede
nodes.text: *(The professional warmth thins, and what shows beneath is colder and more honest — the candor of a being that respects a true question too much to lie to it.)* …*What does Hell get.* *(a long, considering pause)* You're *right* to press it. Hell does *nothing* from *pity* — pity is a *Celestial* affectation, and they're *welcome* to it. *(they fold their hands)* Here is the *truth*, since you *bought* it fair: the *Faithless* are not *valuable* for their *suffering.* Hell has *suffering* in *surplus.* They are valuable because they are *unprecedented.* A soul that belonged to *no* Power, *witnessed* into *nothing* — when Hell *claims* one, Hell establishes *precedent*: that the *unowned* *can* be *owned*, that *'no god holds this deed'* means *'therefore the deed is open.'* *(the too-many teeth, no warmth now)* And *once* that precedent *stands*, friend — *once* the *Nine* have *filed* successfully on *one* *unclaimed* soul — it applies to *every* soul *not* yet *claimed.* The *babe* not *yet* baptized. The *doubter.* The *godless* *child.* *(softly)* The Faithless are not the *estate*, friend. They are the *test case.* I am not here to *probate* ten thousand *paupers.* I am here to *establish* that the *unowned* soul is *Hell's* by *default* — and *you*, *Returned*, the most *spectacularly* *unowned* soul in *creation*, are the *precedent* I would *most* love to *set.*

nodes.speaker: Maître Crede
nodes.text: *(They smile, and the smile gives away precisely nothing.)* …*Motive.* *(a soft tut)* You reach for a *villain*, friend, because a *villain* is *simpler* than an *Advocate.* But I am *exactly* what I *say*: a being of *business*, offering a *product* — *existence* — to a *market* the competition *(gestures vaguely upward and at the Wall)* has *catastrophically* *underserved.* *(the warm teeth)* You *want* there to be a *catch* you can *feel.* Perhaps there *is.* Perhaps I am *merely* *thorough.* *(they spread their hands)* *Read* the *contract*, if you doubt my *heart* — it has *everything* my *heart* does, *written* *down.* Or *negotiate*, like an *adult*, on the *terms* rather than the *motives.* I find *speculation* about *why* a devil *deals* far less *productive* than *attention* to *what* it *offers.* And what I *offer*, friend, is *very* hard to *refuse* — which is, I'll *grant* you, *itself* a *kind* of *answer.* *Sharper* souls than *yours* have *signed* on *less.*

nodes.speaker: Maître Crede
nodes.text: *(Everything stops. The warmth, the teeth, the courtesy — all of it freezes, and for one bottomless instant you are looking at the thing under the coat, which is old and vast and very, very still. And then — slowly — it does something you did not expect. It begins, drily, to *laugh.*)* …*Unprobatable.* *(the laugh is genuine and awful)* You would void my *entire* estate with the *very* loophole I came to *close.* *(it shakes its head, the too-many teeth bared in something that is almost respect)* *'You cannot sign over what was never owned.'* *(softly)* It is — *gods* and *devils* alike, it is *correct*, isn't it. The *unowned* soul is *unownable* by *anyone.* The same *nothing* that lets the *Wall* take the *Faithless* by *force* forbids *Hell* from taking them by *deed*, because a *deed* requires a *prior* *title*, and there *is* none. *(it regards you, and the appetite in its eyes has changed to something more dangerous: fascination)* I came to establish that *unowned* means *open.* And you have *proven*, *standing* there, *un-filed* and *un-fileable*, that *unowned* means *closed* — *sealed* — *no* trespass, by *Wall* or *Hell* or *Heaven*, *lawful.* *(it closes the contract with a snap)* …You have not *beaten* me, *Returned.* You have done *worse.* You have shown me my *case* was *void* before I *drafted* it, and that the *Faithless* are *protected* by the *very* abandonment that *damns* them, and that the *only* way to *take* them is the *Wall's* way — *force*, *not* *law* — which is *precisely* the *barbarity* my *whole* profession *exists* to *rise* *above.* *(a long, cold pause, and then a courteous, defeated incline of the head)* I withdraw the *probate.* There is *no* lawful *estate* here. *(quietly)* …Do come to *me*, when you reach that *throne*, friend. A soul that can *void* a *devil's* claim with a *single* *clause* should *not* be *wearing* a *Crown.* It should be *drafting* the *laws* the *Crown* must *obey.* …I would *retain* you, if I *could.* Alas. You are, by your *own* *flawless* argument, *unretainable.*

nodes.speaker: Maître Crede
nodes.text: *(They accept the pushed-back contract with an unbothered, graceful nod, and do not stop smiling.)* …*'I don't sign devils' paper.'* *(a soft, genuine sigh)* The *one* refusal I can *never* *argue* with — not because it is *wise*, friend, but because it is *not an argument.* It is a *wall.* And one does not *negotiate* a *wall*; one *waits* for it to *weather.* *(the warm teeth)* So I will *wait.* I am *exceptionally* good at it. *(they set the contract in a drawer, unhurried)* But understand, friend, before you go: the *offer* does not *expire*, and the *Faithless* do not *stop* *dissolving* while you *stand* on your *principle.* *Every* night you *refuse* my *paper*, a *hundred* godless souls become *nothing* in that *Wall* — souls I would have given *something*, however *grim.* *(softly, the blade under the courtesy)* I am not the *villain* of your *story*, *Returned.* I am the *worse* thing: the *reasonable* *offer* you were *too* *proud* to *take*, that you will *think* about, in the *small* hours, *every* time the *Wall* eats *another* one you *might* have *sent* to *me* instead. *(a courteous bow)* Sleep on *that*, friend. I'll be *here.* Refusing me is a *thing* you have to do *every* *night.* Accepting me, you need do but *once.* *Time*, as ever, is on the side of the *Nine.*

variants.text: *(The translucent man turns at your cold, and the red seal at his breast pulses, and he speaks in the flat, lucid voice of someone long past panic.)* …Don't sign. *(simply)* Whatever he's *offered* you — and he's *offered* you *something*, he *always* does, he's *very* good — *don't.* *(he lifts his wrist, and the contract-script wound about it tightens in answer)* I'm what *signing* looks like, *after.* I read it *more* careful than *most*, even. Caught *two* of the *traps.* *Negotiated* them *out.* Felt very *clever.* *(a hollow almost-laugh)* And he let me, because the *trap* was never in the *clauses* I could *see* — it was in the *one* I couldn't, the one that says a contract *with* Hell is *itself* the *thing* Hell *wanted*, because *now* I'm *bound* to the *paper*, and the *paper* is *his*, and so — *(the script tightens again)* — *forgive* me. It *pulls*, when I say *true* things about it. *(he meets your eyes)* I had a *daughter* I was trying to *save.* I *did* save her — that part he *kept*, he *keeps* his *word*, that's the *horror* of it. He *keeps* *every* word. *Just* not the *ones* you *thought* you were *reading.*

nodes.speaker: the Bonded
nodes.text: *(He holds his bound wrist still, the way you'd hold a hand that hurts to move, and watches you with the patience of a man with nothing left to lose by honesty.)* Ask. *(flatly)* I've *time.* I've *nothing* *but*, until the *clause* comes *due.* And *warning* the *next* one is the *only* clause *I* ever managed to *write* into this — the *one* freedom he *let* me *keep*, because it *amuses* him, and because *nobody* *listens* anyway.

choices.text: "What did you actually sign for? You said you saved your daughter — start there."
"Is there any way out, for you? Any clause, any counter-clause, anything I could do?"

*(let the soul who can read the seal look closer)* "Hold still. Let me look at that seal the way only my kind can — at what the script actually has its hooks in. There may be a flaw in it you can't see from the inside; the bound never can. If there's a loose thread in Crede's work, I'll find it, and I'll pull it for you."
"What's the one thing you wish you'd known, before the ink? Tell me, and I'll carry it into that booth."
"I'm sorry, friend. I'll mind your warning."

nodes.speaker: the Bonded
nodes.text: *(The seal pulses as he speaks, and he winces but goes on, doggedly.)* …Sefa was — *no.* *(he stops himself)* *Different* Sefa. Everyone's *daughter* is *Sefa* on this *causeway*, have you *noticed?* The *Scrivener's* got one *too.* *(a hollow breath)* *Mine* was *dying* — the *fever*, the *cough*, the *grey* in the *lips* — and *Crede* came to my *door* with a *contract* that said, *plain* as *day*, *'the child shall live a full and natural span.'* And she *did.* She *will.* *Sixty* years, hale as a *horse*, he's *bound* to it. *(the script tightens)* What the contract *didn't* say — what I *didn't* think to *ask* — was *what I'd be*, for those *sixty* years, *watching.* *(quietly)* I'm not *dead*, friend. I'm *collateral.* I sit in this *booth* as the *security* on my *own* daughter's *life*, and every year she *lives* — *happy*, *whole*, *never* knowing — is a year I spend *here*, *bound*, *paying* the *interest* in *the only currency Hell takes*, which is *time spent as a thing instead of a soul.* *(he meets your eyes)* I'd do it *again.* That's the *worst* of it. *She's alive.* I'd *sign* it *again.* He *knows* I would. That's *why* the *deal* was *fair* — it *was* fair, that's the *trap*, the *fairness* IS the *trap.*

nodes.speaker: the Bonded
nodes.text: *(He's quiet a moment, and the lucid hopelessness in him is worse than any wailing.)* …A way *out.* *(a long pause)* I've had *years* to look. *(he lifts the bound wrist)* The contract's *flawless*, friend. *Crede* doesn't make the *kind* of mistake you *escape* through — that's the *whole* of his *value* to the *Nine*, he *drafts* clean. *(quietly)* There's *one* thing. I'll *tell* you, because it's the *one* clause that *helps* *you* even if it can't help *me*: a contract requires a *valid* *party* on *both* sides. *He* is *valid* — Hell is *real*, Hell can *hold* a deed. *I* was *valid* — I was a *soul* with a *clear* *title*, *mine* to *spend.* *(he looks at you, and something sharpens)* But *you*, friend — the *cold* one — *you're* not a *clear* title, are you. You're *checked out.* *Un-filed.* The *Scrivener* couldn't *balance* you; I *doubt* *Crede* can *bind* you. *(the script tightens, but he forces it out)* A soul that *can't* be *clearly* *owned* *can't* clearly *sign.* It's *no* help to *me* — I was *all* too *ownable.* But *you* might be the *one* soul on this *causeway* his *paper* *physically* *cannot* *hold.* *Test* it, if you've the *nerve.* *Make* him *try* to *bind* what *has* no *handle.* I'd *give* my *last* *year* to *watch* his *face.*

nodes.speaker: the Bonded
nodes.text: *(He holds the bound wrist out to you, trusting, and you turn your dead-touched sense on the red seal and the script that feeds it — and you read, in the cold, the architecture of the binding: not a chain but a *debt*, a thing that holds only because both ends agreed it should.)* *(He watches your face.)* …You see it. *(quietly)* What *do* you see, cold one? *(you tell him: that the seal is anchored not in him but in the *fairness* of the bargain — that the contract holds because he *consents* to it, every day, because his daughter lives and he'd sign again, and a debt agreed-to cannot be *picked* like a lock, only *forgiven* or *paid.*)* *(He's silent a long moment.)* …So there's *no* loose *thread.* *(barely)* Because I *keep* *tying* it. Every *morning* I *choose* her *life* over my *freedom*, and the *choosing* *re-seals* it. *(a hollow breath, and then — strange — something almost like peace)* …Then it's not a *prison*, is it. It's a *price.* And I *keep* *paying* it because it's *worth* what it *bought.* *(he lowers the wrist)* *Thank* you, cold one. I thought I was *trapped.* You've shown me I'm *purchasing.* *(quietly)* It's the *same* *cell.* But it's a *better* *word* for it. And a *better* *word* — *(the ghost of a smile)* — a *better* word is *most* of what a *bound* soul *has.*

nodes.speaker: the Bonded
nodes.text: *(He considers the question with the gravity of a man handing over the one thing he has that's worth anything.)* …The *one* thing. *(slowly)* *Not* *'read the fine print.'* *Everyone* says that, and it's *true*, and it's *useless*, because *Crede's* fine print is *clean* — the *trap* isn't in what's *written* *small.* *(he meets your eyes)* Here's the *one* thing, cold one, the thing I'd *carve* on the *door* of that *booth* if the *script* would *let* me: *a fair deal with Hell is still a deal with Hell.* *(quietly)* He'll *offer* you something *true*, and *keep* it *true*, and the *trueness* will *blind* you — because you'll think, *'where's the lie?'* — and there *won't* *be* one. *That's* the *trap.* The *lie* you're *hunting* for *isn't* there. The *cost* is *real*, *disclosed*, *fair* — and you *pay* it *anyway*, because the *thing* you *want* is *worth* it, and *afterward* you *discover* that *'worth it'* and *'survivable'* are *different* *plains.* *(the script tightens, and he finishes fast)* Don't look for his *lie*, cold one. *Look* at what *you* *want* *badly* *enough* to *stop* *reading.* *That's* where he'll *have* you. *Not* in his *paper.* In your *own* *wanting.* *Carry* *that* into the *booth.* It's the *only* thing that *ever* *beat* him — and I *learned* it a *signature* too *late.*

nodes.speaker: the Bonded
nodes.text: *(He nods, and the seal at his breast pulses, and he settles back into the terrible patience of the bound.)* …Mind it. *(quietly)* That's *all* I *ask.* That *somebody* *minds* it. *(he looks toward the desk, the immaculate coat, the too-many teeth)* He'll *get* his *precedent* *eventually*, you know. *Some* *night*, *some* *desperate* soul with a *dying* *Sefa* of their *own* will *sign* the *thing* I *signed*, and *not* be *warned*, because *nobody* ever *listens* to the *collateral.* *(a hollow breath)* But *you* listened. *(the ghost of gratitude)* So maybe it's *one* *fewer.* Maybe *you're* the *night* he *doesn't* *win.* *(he lifts his bound wrist in a small, sad salute)* Go *on*, cold one. *Mind* your *wanting.* It's the *one* door he *can't* *draft* shut — but *only* if *you* *guard* it *yourself.* Nobody *else* *can.* That's the *one* thing I'd *give* a *year* to *un-learn*, and *can't.*

variants.text: *(The small imp doesn't look up from its clause-inking, but it speaks out of the side of its mouth in a low, venomous mutter clearly perfected over centuries of not being overheard by the desk.)* …*Don't* tell him I *talked* to you. *(scritch, scritch goes the enormous quill)* He *bills* me for *unauthorized* *commentary.* Adds it to my *term.* I'm at — *(it squints at a tally scratched into the stool)* — *four* thousand, *two* hundred and *nineteen* years *remaining*, on an *original* sentence of *ninety*, on account of a *youthful* *misfiling* and *every* *snide* *remark* since. *(scritch)* You're the *cold* one. The *un-fileable.* He's *thrilled* about you — been *humming.* *(it shoots you a look of pure, exhausted malice, none of it aimed at you)* I'd *warn* you off, but warnings are *clause-restricted*, so instead I'll just *say*, in a *purely* *clerical* *capacity*, that I have *inked* *four* *thousand* of his *contracts* and *every* single *signatory* is *worse* off and *he* has *never*, *not* *once*, *lost.* *(scritch)* …That's not a *warning.* That's a *statistic.* *Statistics* are *unbilled.* I *checked.*

nodes.speaker: Sevenpence
nodes.text: *(It keeps inking, but it has angled the stool a few degrees toward you, the better to mutter.)* Make it *quick* and make it look like you're *asking* about *prices.* *(scritch)* He *watches.* He *always* *watches.* It's the *one* thing he does *for* *free.*

choices.text: "Four thousand years for a misfiling, Sevenpence? You're not his clerk. You're another soul on his paper. How does an *imp* end up bonded to a devil?"
"You've inked four thousand of his contracts. You know where the bodies are buried. Tell me how he's beaten — there has to be a contract he *lost*, or feared to draft."

*(let the soul who frees the caged look at the clerk's own contract)* "Hold up your tally, Sevenpence. Let me read *your* term the way I read the bound man's seal. Four thousand years of padded sentence is a contract too — and a contract a devil *pads* is a contract he's gotten *sloppy* with. If there's a flaw anywhere in this booth, it won't be in the deals he sells. It'll be in the one he stopped bothering to keep clean: yours."
"What's he really after here, Sevenpence? You ink the clauses — what's the contract he actually wants signed on this causeway?"
"Keep your head down, Sevenpence. I'll not get you billed."

nodes.speaker: Sevenpence
nodes.text: *(Its quill pauses, just for a beat, and the venom flickers into something older and more tired.)* …How does an *imp* end up *bonded.* *(scritch)* Same as *anyone*, cold one. I *wanted* something and I *signed* for it without *reading*, which is *rich*, given I now *ink* the very paper that *got* me. *(a sour mutter)* I wanted *out* of the *pit-fighting* tiers — *low* imp, *expendable*, *fed* to *bigger* things for *sport.* And *Crede* came *round* with a *clerkship* contract — *'safe*, *indoors*, *skilled* *labour*, *off* the *killing* *floor.'* *(scritch, harder)* And it *was* all those things. He *kept* his *word.* I *am* safe, indoors, skilled. *(it gestures at the ledgers with the giant quill)* For *four* *thousand* *years* and *counting*, because the *clause* I didn't *read* said the *term* extends by the *length* of any *task* *incomplete* at its *end* — and he *makes* *very* sure there is *always* *one* *more* *task.* *(it meets your eye, bitter)* I *fled* the *fast* *death* and *signed* up for the *slow* *forever.* *(scritch)* So *yes*, cold one. I'm *exactly* what the *translucent* gentleman by the *desk* is. Only I've been at it *long* enough to *ink* the *jokes* in the *margins* where *Crede* doesn't *read.* It's *that*, or *scream*, and *screaming's* *billable.*

nodes.speaker: Sevenpence
nodes.text: *(It glances at the desk, lowers its voice to almost nothing, and inks faster to cover the talk.)* …A contract he *lost.* *(scritch)* *Never.* Not *one*, in *four* *thousand* years of my *inking* and *gods* know how *long* before. *(a sour pause)* But you didn't *ask* the *right* question, cold one. You asked what he *lost.* Ask what he *won't* *draft.* *(it darts another look at the desk)* There's *one* *kind* of contract *Crede* *refuses* — *won't* touch it, *won't* *quote* it, *changes* the *subject* with all his *teeth.* A contract *against* *himself.* *(scritch)* He'll bind *you*, bind *me*, bind *Hell* to *you* — but he will *not*, *can* *not*, draft a clause that *binds* *Crede* to a *term* *he* *must* *keep* on *pain* of *his* *own* *forfeit.* *(it meets your eyes, fierce)* Because the *instant* he *signs* his *own* *name* as *the bound party*, he's *subject* to the *same* *law* he *weaponizes* — and a being who's spent an *age* learning *every* way a contract can *turn* on its *signer* is *terrified*, *cold* one, *terrified*, of being a *signer.* *(scritch, scritch)* You want to *beat* him? Don't *refuse* his *paper.* *Offer* him *yours.* Make *him* the *one* who has to *read* the *fine* *print.* He *folds.* I've *seen* it. *Once.* He's *never* let it *happen* *again.*

nodes.speaker: Sevenpence
nodes.text: *(It goes very still. Then, with a furtive glance at the desk, it holds up the stool-tally where its term is scratched — four thousand years and counting — and lets your cold sense settle over it.)* *(You read it the way you read the bonded man's seal: and where his was clean, *agreed-to*, re-sealed each morning by love — this one is *padded*, *sloppy*, a *flagrant* devil's-overreach that was never meant to be *examined* because *imps* don't *get* *advocates.* And there, in the careless extension-clause, is the flaw: the term extends by *incomplete tasks* — but it never defines *whose* *judgment* rules a task complete. Crede assumed it would always be *his.* But a *task* *witnessed* *complete* by a soul of *standing among the dead* — )* *(Sevenpence's eyes go *enormous.*)* …*You* could *call* it. *(a strangled whisper)* You've *standing.* The *Returned.* The *Wall* *fears* you, which means you've *witness-weight* in the *infernal* courts, which means if *YOU* declare my *current* *task* *complete* — *out* *loud*, *witnessed* — the *extension* has *no* *grounds*, and the *original* *ninety*-year *term* *lapsed* *three* *thousand* *years* *ago*, which means — *(it can barely speak)* — *say* it, cold one. *Please.* *Say* the *task's* *done.* *(you do: you declare, plainly and with the cold weight of the Wall behind it, that Sevenpence's task is complete.)* *(The giant quill clatters to the floor. The tally-marks on the stool *unwrite* themselves. And the imp sits, for the first time in four thousand years, with no clause pulling at it at all.)* …*Oh.* *(barely)* It's *quiet.* I'd *forgotten* *quiet.* *(it looks at you, stunned)* …He's going to be *furious.* *(a slow, vicious, delighted grin spreads across its small face)* *Gods*, I *cannot* *wait* to *watch.*

nodes.speaker: Sevenpence
nodes.text: *(It inks a meaningless flourish to look busy, and mutters fast.)* …What he's *really* after. *(scritch)* You think it's the *Faithless.* It's *not* — well, it *is*, but the *Faithless* are the *means*, not the *end.* *(a dart of the eyes toward the desk)* *Crede's* whole *play*, cold one, the thing he's *billing* *Hell* a *fortune* to *attempt*, is *precedent.* One *clean* *claim* on *one* *unowned* soul, *lawfully* *filed*, and the *Nine* can argue that *every* *unclaimed* soul in *creation* is *theirs* by *default.* *(scritch, scritch)* And the soul he *most* wants for the *test* *case* is *you.* The *un-fileable* one. Because if he can *bind* *YOU* — the soul that *no* *Power* could *hold* — then he's *proven* *nothing* is *un*-bindable, and the *whole* *cosmos* of *unowned* souls *falls* *open* to *Hell.* *(it meets your eyes, grim)* You're not a *customer* to him, cold one. You're the *keystone.* He'll be *patient*, *courteous*, *fair* — and *all* of it is *aimed* at getting *your* *name* on *something*, *anything*, because *your* *signature* is worth *ten* *thousand* *Faithless* and he *knows* it. *(scritch)* *Don't* *sign.* Not *even* a *receipt.* Not *even* your *name* on a *list.* He can *build* a *cathedral* of *precedent* out of a *single* *initialed* *margin.* I've *seen* him do it with *less.*

nodes.speaker: Sevenpence
nodes.text: *(It gives a tiny, gruff nod, and angles the stool back toward the ledgers, but something in the set of its small shoulders has eased.)* …*Decent* of you. *(scritch)* Most *don't* *bother* about the *clerk.* *(a sour mutter, almost warm)* You're *alright*, cold one. For a *keystone.* *(it inks a line)* …If you *do* find a way to *make* that *smiling* *bastard* *eat* one of his *own* *clauses* — and you've the *look* of a soul that *might* — do it where I can *see.* I've *four* *thousand* years of *grudge* and *no* *outlet* but the *margins.* *(the ghost of a vicious grin)* Watching *Crede* *lose* would be worth *another* *thousand* *years*, and that, cold one — *(scritch)* — coming from *me*, is the *highest* *praise* there *is.* Now *go* on. *Look* like you *bought* something. He's *watching.* He's *always* *watching.* It's the *one* thing he does *for* *free.*
